Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Sharon Yeter For Attorneys Fees; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Aug-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert Mezzetti, 408.279.8400 of Mezzetti Law Firm, Inc. for Plaintiff Eleanor Young appearing via Zoom.
Jim Burns, 415.392.2018 of Stimmel, Stimmel and Roeser for Defendant Sharon Yeter as Trustee of the Cohn Trust Created November 19, 1996 appearing via Zoom.
Amy Harringto, 415.558.7700 Partitian Referee appearing via Zoom.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SHARON YETER Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Sharon Yeter For Attorneys Fees is DENIED. Moving party seeks attorney's fees based on a contract to which Mr. Lindley was not a party. No authority is cited for the proposition that Mr. Lindley's share should be reduced by the amount of attorney's fees incurred by Young and Yeter because Young and Yeter had an agreement to that effect. No other basis for recovery of fees is cited. The Court further notes that the relief sought (award of fees) is inconsistent with the Court's January 21, 2021 order and appears to be subject to CCP 1008, which is not addressed in the moving papers. Furthermore, had the Court been aware of the existence of the Young-Yeter agreement and their claims to be reimbursed for fees out of Lindley's share at January 21, 2021 the Court would have been concerned about equity (CCP 874.010) and reasonableness of $80,000 in cumulative fees in this uncontested partition action. Finally, the Court has now become aware that Mr. Lindley died and propriety of proceeding in his absence with an award of fees under a contract that was not the basis for the Complaint upon which the default was entered, is not addressed by the moving defendant. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Nov-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Jearl R. Lindley, as Trustee of the Jearl R. and Annabelle Lindley Trust.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Patricia Dowling, CSR #5388. Email: pdowling@sftc.org.

Robert L. Mezzetti II, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Eleanor Young, who was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Young appeared telephonically for this morning's proceedings.
Ronak Patel, Esq. appeared with defendant Sharon Yeter, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Jearl R. Lindley,as Trustee of the Jearl R. and Annabelle Lindley Trust.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Partition was filed on September 13, 2017.
Amendment to plaintiff's complaint substituting Jearl R. Linldey, as Trustee of the Jearl R. and Annabelle Lindley Trust for DOE Number 1 was filed on July 26, 2018.
Default as to defendant Jearl R. Linldey, as Trustee of the Jearl R. and Annabelle Lindley Trust, was entered on September 17, 2018.
Settlement Agreement between plaintiff Eleanor Young and defendant Sharon Yeter was entered on April 2, 2019.
DOE defendants 2 through 20 were dismissed on July 3, 2019.
Dismissal without prejudice of defendant the Testate and Intestate Successors of Annabell Lindley, deceased, was filed today, November 16, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Eleanor Young, and against the defaulted defendant Jearl R. Lindley,as Trustee of the Jearl R. and Annabelle Lindley Trust, as follows:

(1) Eleanor Young, individually, has an undivided one-third interest in the property located at 374-376 11th Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94118 (hereinafter "the property");
(2) Sharon Yeter, as Trustee of the Cohen Trust Created on November 19, 1996, has an undivided one-third interest in the property;
(3) Jearl R. Lindley, as Trustee of the Jearl R. and Annabell Lindley Trust has an undivided on-third interest in the property;
(4) Tim Johnson of Paragon Realty Group is appointed Referee;
(5) The property shall be sold only as an entire unit;
(6) The property shall be put on multiple listings to be sold at the highest market price possible; and
(7) The Referee, after making the sale of such property, is hereby directed to to report the Courtr all preceedings thereon, on May 7, 2020.

Additionally, the Court awards plaintiff attorney fees in the amount of $39,189.95 and costs in the amount of $2,749.95.

Proposed Interlocutory Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
